name: Connes Embedding Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The universe of all von Neumann algebras and quantum complexity classes

VINIT.justification: The uninitialized, unclassified von Neumann algebra before any embedding or trace is defined.

TANCH.justification: The ultimate containing boundary; the target space that bounds all embeddable factors.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism mapping the abstract factor into the hyperfinite ultrapower.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism projecting sequences from the ultrapower back to the finite factor to analyze embedding failures.

CLINK.justification: The composition of two operator algebras into a joint quantum system.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity $R \cong R \otimes R$, the factor recognizing its own infinite tensor structure.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the abstract tensor product into minimal (spatial) and maximal completions.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the abstract tensor product by identifying min and max norms as a single unique C*-norm.

EVALT.justification: The positive evaluation where the factor embeds and the minimal norm equals the maximal norm.

EVALF.justification: The negative evaluation where the factor fails to embed and the minimal norm strictly diverges from the maximal norm.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice where the mathematical community held both embeddability and non-embeddability as unresolved possibilities simultaneously.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, irreversible 2020 resolution fixing the negative answer in the mathematical record.

phase_3.void_description: Unclassified II_1 factor, no trace or embedding defined yet.
phase_3.true_description: Factor successfully embedded in $R^\omega$, Tsirelson's problem resolved affirmatively for this system.
phase_3.false_description: Factor proven non-embeddable, MIP*=RE counterexample realized, Tsirelson resolved negatively.
phase_3.both_description: The unresolved conjecture state, where embeddability is neither proven nor disproven, held in mathematical tension.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the abstract type II_1 von Neumann factor before any embedding or trace is defined.
Establish the hyperfinite II_1 factor's self-similarity ($R \cong R \otimes R$), the core identity of the target space.
Set the hyperfinite ultrapower $R^\omega$ as the terminal anchor and universal containing boundary for embeddable factors.
Bifurcate the abstract C*-algebraic tensor product into its minimal (spatial) and maximal norm completions.
Anchor the true branch, representing the affirmative case where the minimal norm equals the maximal norm (Tsirelson affirmative).
Apply the forward trace-preserving embedding morphism mapping the factor into the hyperfinite ultrapower $R^\omega$.
Anchor the false branch, representing the negative case where the norms diverge (MIP*=RE counterexample).
Apply the reverse ultraproduct limit descent, analyzing the failure to embed by pulling back complexity bounds from the ultrapower.
Reconstitute the abstract C*-tensor product by identifying the minimal and maximal completions, satisfying the Frobenius constraint.
Enter the pre-2020 paradice, holding both the Connes conjecture (embeddability) and its negation in unresolved mathematical tension.
Compose the quantum multiprover interactive proof class (MIP*) with the recursively enumerable class (RE) to form the equivalence MIP*=RE.
Permanently fix the 2020 Ji-Natarajan-Vidick-Wright-Yuen proof in the mathematical record, irreversibly establishing that not all II_1 factors are embeddable.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Mathematical logic and operator algebra framework translating conjectures into formal proofs.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Trace-preserving completely positive maps and bimodule correspondences communicating between von Neumann algebras.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The ultrapower construction $R^\omega$ storing sequences of operators modulo a free ultrafilter.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The free ultrafilter $\omega$ determining the limit convergence and timing of sequences in the ultrapower.
phase_5.alfs_store: The foundational axioms of ZFC set theory and the standard definition of von Neumann algebras.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Mathematical effort and computational complexity (quantum entanglement resources) consumed per proof cycle.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unresolved conjecture state with high informational entropy regarding the embeddability of arbitrary II_1 factors.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Resolved state where MIP*=RE is proven, reducing the entropy of the embeddability question to zero (definitively false).
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because the informational uncertainty is perfectly converted into formal mathematical certainty (proof) without loss of logical rigor.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: Connes Embedding Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def connes_embedding_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the abstract type II_1 von Neumann factor before any embedding or ...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Establish the hyperfinite II_1 factor's self-similarity ($R \cong R \otimes R...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [2] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Set the hyperfinite ultrapower $R^\omega$ as the terminal anchor and universa...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Bifurcate the abstract C*-algebraic tensor product into its minimal (spatial) and maximal norm completions.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the true branch, representing the affirmative case where the minimal n...)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Apply the forward trace-preserving embedding morphism mapping the factor into...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the false branch, representing the negative case where the norms diver...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Apply the reverse ultraproduct limit descent, analyzing the failure to embed ...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute the abstract C*-tensor product by identifying the minimal and maximal completions, satisfying the Frobenius constraint.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter the pre-2020 paradice, holding both the Connes conjecture (embeddabilit...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the quantum multiprover interactive proof class (MIP*) with the recur...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanently fix the 2020 Ji-Natarajan-Vidick-Wright-Yuen proof in the mathema...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def connes_embedding_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (connes_embedding_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def connes_embedding_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (connes_embedding_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem connes_embedding_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
